mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force earnings downgrades and cause rapid negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 60% of contraction periods, or the signal fails to predict returns when industrial production declines are driven by seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with forward returns when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 2.8% year-over-year forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of high-inflation observations, or the threshold effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is adjusted for energy prices.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales indicates a sector rotation away from consumer discretionary stocks, triggering negative broad market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with stable inflation create a favorable discount rate environment that sustains equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns turn negative in over 55% of low-rate stable-inflation periods, or the mechanism breaks down when real yields rise above 2.0%.
